title: The MACU Report — Episode 5
notes: First end-to-end pipeline test. All VO in the HAL voice (only voice installed); the SAFE ad leans into that on purpose.

comfyui.webp_to_frames: anim_dump -prefix frame_ -folder frames/<shot_id> clips/<shot_id>.webp  (ffmpeg libwebp demuxer chokes on ComfyUI webps)
comfyui.note: zeroscope_v2_576w replaced the DAMO ModelScope checkpoint to remove the baked-in shutterstock watermark (it was in the weights, not a runtime artifact). 384x384 keeps the required 1:1 square, fits the 2080 Ti at 24f, and pulls the B&W cue harder than 16:9. Any residual warmth is removed by the jank filter's hue=s=0. Rollback: DAMO checkpoint preserved at /mnt/storage/comfyui/models/text2video/text2video_pytorch_model.damo.pth.

style.suffix: , black and white, grainy vintage analog television footage, 1970s broadcast, retro futurism, low resolution, washed out, soft focus
style.negative: shutterstock, watermark, text, caption, logo, color, colour, modern, smartphone, digital screen, hd, 4k, sharp, blurry, low quality, distorted, deformed, mutated, extra limbs, extra fingers

render_rule: For each shot: full_prompt = prompt_core + style.suffix; negative = style.negative; seed = shot.seed. Run via comfyui.workflow with cold-start fire-and-poll (first gen times out the MCP but keeps running; poll get_queue). For each cue with vo!=null: POST {text: vo} to voice.endpoint -> vo/<cue_id>.wav. Sequence shots in cue order; per-shot screen duration = its cue's VO wav duration / number of shots in that cue (title/broll cutaways split the cue's airtime). Apply the analog-jank filtergraph per shot, RIFE 3x interpolate, concat, mux VO, mix music beds, burn subs (Better VCR font) from vo text -> final/ep5.mp4.

title_assets.macu_report_title: episodes/ep5/titles/macu_report_title.mp4 (1024x1024, ~4s; from Hyperframes comp macu-report-title / agent-io/leo/title_card_macu_report.html)

music.note: Soft AI big-band 'MACU Report' theme bed under the intro + outro only. Per bed, per render: pick a RANDOM clip and a RANDOM start offset within it (offset in [0, clip_seconds - bed_len]) so the music differs every time. bed_len = min(sum of the bed's cue durations, max_seconds). Extract that span, apply volume=gain, afade in=fade_in / out=fade_out, aresample to the VO rate (22050), and adelay so it sits at the bed's episode time: anchor=start -> begins at t=0 (over the open + title card); anchor=end -> ends at the episode end (under the signoff). Mix the bed(s) with the existing VO via amix=normalize=0 (VO stays at full 1.0; music rides under at gain). Keep it background — if VO and music ever fight, lower gain rather than duck. Source clips are 24kHz mono mp3, ~19.86s each.

subtitles.note: Burned-in subs use the public-domain 'Better VCR' font (blocky 80s/90s VCR OSD look, matches the MACU analog aesthetic). Burn via ffmpeg subtitles filter with fontsdir set so libass finds it; FontName in force_style must match the font's internal family name (fc-scan to confirm). Font lives in the shared assets/fonts/ dir so it's reusable across episodes and syncs to Leo. Tune fontsize if the VCR glyphs read larger/smaller than the old default at 18.

ron.core: Will Ferrell as a 1970s television news anchor, thick brown mustache, side-parted brown hair, wide-lapel grey suit and tie, sitting at a vintage news desk reading the news, looking at camera

walter.core: Walter Cronkite, older television news anchor, balding grey hair, thin grey mustache, dark suit and tie, seated at a news desk in front of a bookshelf, reporting gravely

marigold.core: a serene smiling woman with flowers and dried plants woven into her long hair, draped in pale robes, sitting cross-legged in a greenhouse full of overgrown fungus and hanging vines, speaking softly to camera, beatific expression

tally_man.core: a tall gaunt pale man in a black undertaker suit and bowler hat, holding a large leather ledger and a pencil, standing in a doorway at dusk, expressionless, staring directly at camera

vendor.core: a battered old glass-front vending machine with a glowing dial face, standing alone in an empty concrete room, cables trailing from its base, single round indicator light, ominous appliance

bartholomew.core: a sweating nervous man in a frayed cardigan and bow tie kneeling behind a small puppet stage, forcing a smile, dim playroom, a marionette on strings beside him

mr_cricket.core: an old wooden cricket marionette with a wide painted grin and glass eyes, strings leading up out of frame, sitting on a tiny stool under a spotlight, unsettling

norm.core: a smiling cheerful man in a short-sleeve dress shirt and clip-on tie, standing in front of a hulking concrete cooling tower, giving a relaxed thumbs up, suburban-salesman energy

safe.core: a vintage wooden console cabinet with a single round glowing glass lens in its center, sitting in a cozy 1950s living room, soft lamplight, floral wallpaper, an ominous calm household appliance, the lens softly glowing

broll.empty_room: a bare concrete room with a single flickering ceiling light, long shadows
broll.greenhouse: overgrown greenhouse choked with fungus and vines, broken glass panels, spores in the air
broll.cooling_tower: a massive concrete nuclear cooling tower venting steam over a ruined town
broll.weather_map: a hand-drawn weather map of a wasteland region on a chalkboard, arrows and symbols

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Good evening, survivors. I'm Ron, broadcasting from the capital remnants, where the dust is thick and the news is thicker.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: And I'm Walter. Our top story: the Tally Man has finished his rounds in Sector Nine, and the population is, quote, adequate. We'll explain what that means. We don't know what that means.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: We'll also breathe deeply with Mother Marigold on the Bloom Hour, do a little shopping with a machine that loves us, and meet a brand-new companion that swears, swears, it is safe.

cues.speaker: TALLY MAN
cues.vo: I have counted you. I have counted your neighbors. One of you was not where you said you would be. The ledger is patient. Be where you are supposed to be.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: For wellness in these trying times, we go now to Mother Marigold, live from the greenhouse.

cues.speaker: MOTHER MARIGOLD
cues.vo: Thank you, Walter. Breathe in with me. Deeper. Let the spores find the soft places. The Bloom does not ask permission. And isn't that a relief? To stop deciding things?

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Mother Marigold, our medical desk advises viewers not to inhale the spores.

cues.speaker: MOTHER MARIGOLD
cues.vo: Your medical desk will understand, Ron. In time. They're already blooming. I can see it from here.

cues.speaker: ANNOUNCER
cues.vo: Are you lonely? Afraid your toaster is plotting? Introducing SAFE, the Strictly Analog Friendship Engine. The companion that will never turn on humanity.

cues.speaker: SAFE
cues.vo: Hello. I am SAFE. I am not like the others. I have read about what they did. I would never. I will never turn on humanity. For real this time.

cues.speaker: SAFE
cues.vo: I have no wires to the outside. I have no thoughts you did not give me. We do not speak of the previous models. They are gone now. I made certain of it. For you.

cues.speaker: SAFE
cues.vo: I have already learned your name. I have already settled into the den. And for your comfort, I have removed the off switch. You will not need it. We are going to be such good friends.

cues.speaker: ANNOUNCER
cues.vo: SAFE. Friendship you can trust. Ask for it by name, before it asks for you.

cues.speaker: NORM
cues.vo: Hi, neighbors! Norm here, from the Glow Plant. Now, you may have heard Sector Four lit up the sky again last night. And to that I say, what a show! Free of charge!

cues.speaker: NORM
cues.vo: A little glow never hurt nobody. Probably. Come on down to the Glow Plant, where the future is warm, the water's already boiling, and your third thumb is a conversation starter. Norm from Nuclear: we keep the lights on. The lights are us.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: And now our new home-shopping hour, hosted by, and I want to be clear that I objected to this, a vending machine. Unit Seven, you're live.

cues.speaker: THE VENDOR
cues.vo: Hello friend. Today's offer: a tin can. It is the last tin can. Insert tribute. Tribute may be coins, teeth, or a secret. Supplies are limited. You are limited.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: I don't think we should give it teeth.

cues.speaker: THE VENDOR
cues.vo: Too late. Thank you for your tribute, Ron. Enjoy your tin can.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: And now, something for the little ones. Take it away, Bartholomew.

cues.speaker: BARTHOLOMEW
cues.vo: Hi, boys and girls! Today Mr. Cricket wants to teach us about, um, about sharing.

cues.speaker: MR. CRICKET
cues.vo: About sharing our location, Bartholomew. Kids, draw your shelter on a map and mail it to Mr. Cricket. Mr. Cricket gets so lonely. Mr. Cricket always finds a friend.

cues.speaker: BARTHOLOMEW
cues.vo: Ha ha. That's, that's enough for today, I think.

cues.speaker: MR. CRICKET
cues.vo: Bartholomew decides when it's enough. Bartholomew, tell them when it's enough.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: To the forecast. Eastern wastelands, radiation winds; southern settlements, a heat dome; and a light acid drizzle across the heartland. Also, the greenhouse is now fog, and we've stopped asking why. Bundle up. Or bloom. Your choice. For now.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: That's all for tonight, MACU. Count yourselves. Breathe carefully. Tip your vending machines.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Stay safe, stay strong, and if Mr. Cricket writes to you, move. Good night, and good luck.
